characters.name: Cherisha Kimball, Deputy
characters.description: Deputy Cherisha KimballBiographyFull NameCherisha KimballAliasesDeputy KimballStatusAliveGenderFemaleRelativesUnnamed parentsRomancesUnnamed dateOccupationDeputyResidenceReno, NevadaAppearancesSeasons3-5,7-8Episodes75First"Cop School"Last"The Hills Have Owls"Portrayed byMary Birdsong Not much is know about her early life, except that she is originally fromShreveport,Louisianaand, according to her accounts, led a past filled withsinand small time crimes and misdemeanours. She states that she joined the force because she believes "Enforcing the law is my way of righting all my wrongs, cleaning up mykarma. Also, they give good benefits." She joined the force early in Season 3, winning a competition among top police academy graduates; however, she was only chosen after the two front-runners withdrew for various reasons.

characters.name: Jim Ronald Dangle, Lieutenant
characters.description: Lt. Jim DangleBiographyFull NameJim Ronald DangleAliasesLieutenant DangleStatusAliveGenderMaleRelativesFrederick(uncle)RomancesTrudy Wiegel(fling)OccupationLieutenantResidenceReno, NevadaAppearancesSeasons1-8Episodes124First"Pilot"Last"The Hills Have Owls"Portrayed byThomas Lennon Dangle was born and raised in Arkansas. A suspect who reads Dangle's driver's license in Season 1 states that he is 41 years old in the summer of 2003, placing his birth year at late 1961 or early to mid 1962. Dangle's father abandoned his family when he was a child and his mother committedsuicide, after which he was raised by his Uncle Frederick. Meanwhile, Dangle's father moved toChicagoand started a new family. Dangle's father had two more African-American children who appear in Episode 505 to visit Dangle after the death of their father to settle the will. The will turns out to be a bill for the funeral of more than $5,000, and aBanana Splitskeychain that Dangle's half-sister gives to him.

characters.name: Larrie Plum
characters.description: Larrie Plumia an old man in a folding lawn chair who often calls the cops to report that he has a list of people who ran a stop sign or that he's lost his falcon. He is played byKip King. His name is given inNot Without My Mustache.  The odd spelling is due to multiple Larrys in his family.

characters.name: S. Jones, Deputy
characters.description: "And the installation is freeeeee-eeeee!"Deputy S. JonesBiographyFull NameSisyphus Cosman JonesAliasesJonesyDeputy JonesStatusAliveGenderMaleRelativesUnnamed parentsRomancesRaineesha Williams(ex-girlfriend)OccupationDeputyResidenceReno, NevadaAppearancesSeasons1-8Episodes124First"Pilot"Last"The Hills Have Owls"Portrayed byCedric Yarbrough Jones was raised inMound, Minnesota(asuburbof the "Twin Cities" area ofMinneapolis-St. Paul). In a season six episode, it was revealed that Jones was a member of the Reno Sheriff's Department for about 15 years as he is shown in a old video tape of the officers that dates back to the late 1980s. Jones is an emotionally shallow smooth-talker who frequently channels his need for attention into sexually promiscuous behavior. It is implied--typically, by Dep. Williams herself--that he is Raineesha's ex-boyfriend, although he seems to pursue every woman other than her. Despite Williams' jealous crush on him, he has an on-again/off-again,"booty call"-type relationship with Dep. Johnson.

characters.name: Shooting Victim
characters.description: Shooting VictimBiographyStatusUnknownGenderMaleAppearancesFirstHow We Do It In RenoPortrayed bySteve Hart An unnamed officer (credited as "shooting victim") appears in the cold open of thePilotepisode. In the sketch,Deputy James Garciareceives an emergency radio call while patrolling asking for all units to respond to shots fired at 1433 Murray Drive, where an officer is down. Garcia turns on his sirens and lights, speeds to the address, and opens the front gate with his gun drawn. As Garcia enters the dark home and announces his presence, the lights suddenly turn on, and everyone yells "Surprise!" Garcia instinctively fires at the shooting victim, sending him onto his back. The camera shows the officers of theReno Sheriff's Department, includingLieutenant Jim Dangle,Deputy S. Jones,Deputy Raineesha Williams,Deputy Travis Junior,Deputy Clementine Johnson, andDeputy Trudy Wiegel. The shooting victim was also wearing a sheriff's uniform, indicating he works at the Department with the rest of the squad. The room is decorated with balloons and a "Happy 40th Birthday!" banner. As Deputy Garcia sheepishly puts away his gun, Deputy Jones calls in for an officer down at 1433 Murray Drive.
